LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF RON NG'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM AUG-18-2004 TO LAW AND MOTION AT SEP-15-2004, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301. COUNSEL SHALL SUBMIT SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFS NO LATER THAN 08/25/04 WITH COURTESY COPIES PROVIDED TO THE COURT ADDRESSING THE FOLLOWING: 1) GIVEN THAT LIKOM USA HAS FILED BANKRUPTCY, CAN THE COURT GRANT A MOTION TO AMEND; AND (2) A DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY REGARDING THE CIRCUMSTANCES THAT ALLEGEDLY CAUSED THE FILING OF THE MOTION TO AMEND AS OPPOSED TO FILING THE MOTION AT AN EARLIER DATE. IF THE COURT DOES NOT REQUIRE ADDITIONAL ARGUMENT, THE COURT WILL ISSUE A MINUTE ORDER TAKING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Miranda Kolbe, Esq., Schubert & Reed, (415) 788-4220, for Plaintiff and Moving Party
Brian D. Henri, Esq., Legal Department of Fry's Electronics, (408) 487-4712, for Defendant and Opposing Party Fry's Electronics
Ted Lindquist, Esq., Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton, (415) 434-9100, for Defendant and Opposing Party Likom USA, Inc.

RULING:
A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF RON NG'S MOTION For Leave To File First Amended Complaint: GRANTED EXCEPT AS TO LIKOM, INC. Notice waived. First amended complaint must be filed within 10 days from today. Defendant Fry's Electronics' request for cost is denied. Order signed. (301/JLW) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-18-2004 Continued To Sep-15-04 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Miranda Kolbe, Esq., Schubert & Reed LLP (415) 788-4220 for Plaintiff and Moving Party Ng
Ted C. Lindquist, III, Esq., Sheppard Mullin (415) 434-9100 for Defendant and Opposing Party Likom USA, Inc. and Fry's Electronics
Brian D. Henri, Legal Counsel for Defendant and Opposing Party Fry's Electronics (408) 487-4712
RULING:
A R G U E D; Plaintiff Ron Ng's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is CONTINUED to 09/15/04 in Dept. 301. Counsel shall submit simultaneous briefs no later than 08/25/04 with courtesy copies provided to the Court addressing the following: 1) given that Likom USA has filed bankruptcy, can the Court grant a motion to amend; and (2) a declaration under penalty of perjury regarding the circumstances that allegedly caused the filing of the motion to amend as opposed to filing the motion at an earlier date. If the Court does not require additional argument, the Court will issue a minute order taking Plaintiff's motion off calendar. (301/JLW/DS) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
